findings.issue: isSafeReturnTo accepts query-only and fragment-only strings, causing redirect loops. Values like '?error=foo' pass the same-origin check because URL constructor resolves them to the base URL with the new query/fragment. From /oauth/callback, window.location.assign('?error=foo') navigates to /oauth/callback?error=foo, which re-triggers the OAuthCallbackPage's oauthError branch and bounces the user to /login, wasting the OAuth round-trip. A crafted link like /login?return_to=%3Ferror%3Dfoo (or any path the user is on at the time) reproduces this without any cross-origin component.
findings.remediation: Add a path-shape precondition: return parsed.pathname.startsWith('/') and reject strings that are empty or start with '?'/'#'/'\' or contain ':' before the first '/'. Alternatively, replace the URL-based check with parsed.pathname === '/oauth/callback' || parsed.pathname.startsWith('/nodes') etc. for an explicit allowlist of same-origin paths.

findings.issue: API error messages leak raw HTTP status codes and discard backend error bodies. Every API throw constructs a message of the form 'oauth init failed: ${status}', 'oauth redeem failed: ${status}', 'Invitation not found (${status})', 'Failed to accept invitation (${status})' which is then shown directly to end users on the LoginPage error banner and the InvitationCompletePage/InvitationPage error card. The backend's JSON error body is never parsed or surfaced, so a 410 with a useful 'invitation already redeemed, please sign in' message is reduced to 'oauth redeem failed: 410'. This is the user-facing manifestation of the 'discard backend JSON error body' known gap; the fix should target the user-visible message, not just the data layer.
findings.remediation: In each ApiError throw site, attempt to await response.clone().json() and prefer response_data.message / error_data.message when present; fall back to a status-mapped human string (e.g. 410 -> 'This invitation link has already been used') and only show the raw status as a last resort. Keep the status on the ApiError for programmatic checks.

findings.issue: isSafeReturnTo accepts a same-origin bare relative path that resolves against the current page. 'new URL("foo", "http://localhost:3000/oauth/callback")' produces 'http://localhost:3000/oauth/foo' — a same-origin URL the user never intended to land on. If /oauth/foo doesn't exist the user gets a NotFoundPage; if it ever does exist, the OAuth callback page can be tricked into dropping the user onto an arbitrary same-origin route. The current tests only cover leading-slash paths and full URLs, so this branch is untested.
findings.remediation: Require url to start with '/' before falling through to the URL-constructor check (or pre-check that the first character is '/' or a same-origin absolute URL). Add test cases for 'foo', 'foo/bar', and '?x=1' / '#x' alongside the existing assertions.

findings.issue: The OAuthCallbackPage's catch block in AppRouter.tsx:159 uses the legacy 'err instanceof Error || err instanceof DOMException' shape even though DOMException extends Error, so the DOMException arm is dead. Same redundancy exists at InvitationCompletePage.tsx:91 and was already noted as a known code smell, but the OAuthCallbackPage site was added/updated in this workstream and the redundancy was not removed there. Easy cleanup.
findings.remediation: Simplify both error message extractions to 'err instanceof Error ? err.message : <fallback>'. Already filed as a known code smell — bumping severity for the missed cleanup site is the only new content.
